ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Conference
Off Calendar Feb-20-2008 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Conference
Off Calendar Feb-20-2008 Continued To Feb-27-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any party, counsel or interested person.
The Court ordered matter continued for one week to Feb. 27, 2008 for status on mediation, the clerk is to notify counsel for an appearance.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR# 11722. Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, Bailiff: Deputy Handa.

After contacting counsel, the court was made aware that a Dismissal was filed on Nov. 30, 2007, therefore the matter is taken off calendar.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Conference
Off Calendar Feb-13-2008 Continued To Feb-20-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any party, counsel or interested person. The matter was continued to February 20, 2008 for status on the mediation. The clerk will notify counsel to appear.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR# 11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Lewis.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Conference
Off Calendar Nov-28-2007 Continued To Feb-13-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Drexel Bradshaw for Charles Bergeron (Petitioner). Richard Harris for Katherine Zalewski (Respondent).

Mr. Harris reported that a settlement agreeement had been reached, and requested a continuance to mid February as well as a stay on the litigation. The Order For Stay Of Litigation was signed in open court. The matter was continued to February 13, 2008 for status on the settlement.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Lee and Deputy Smith.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Bracamontes for Charles Bergeron (Petitioner).

There being no opposition filed, the tentative ruling granting the Motion For Order Compelling Joinder Of California Public Employee's Retirement System is confirmed (order signed for the same in open court).

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Conference
Off Calendar Oct-31-2007 Continued To Nov-20-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Bracamontes for Charles Bergeron (Petitioner).

Mr. Bracamontes reported that the case was in the Civil Court and that mediation was set for November 14, 2007. He also stated that no accounting had been done, that the real property had been sold, and that the 401K was at issue. He is requesting the Court order an accounting or set the matter for trial. The Court continued the matter to November 20, 2007 for status on mediation.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Doug Handa.

Off Calendar Jun-13-2007 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Response To Requests For Production Of Documents (Set No. Three), And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-26-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearance:
Michael R. Bracamontes, Esq., for Petitioner, Estate of Jospeh McLaughlin Living Trust
Richard Harris, Esq., for Respondent Kathy Zalewski

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Respondent and Cross-Complainant Katherine A. Zalewski's Motion To Compel Response To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Three, is granted. Petitioner shall respond and produce documents within 20 days of notice of this order. Objections are overruled with the exception of claims of privilege. Any documents withheld pursuant to a claim of privilege shall be identified with particularity. The court finds that the failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded against Petitioner in the amount of $900, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Impose Issue Sanctions, Evidentiary Sanctions; And Monetary Sanctions Against Petitioner Charles Bergeron
Off Calendar Feb-26-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearance:
Michael R. Bracamontes, Esq., for Petitioner, Estate of Jospeh McLaughlin Living Trust
Richard Harris, Esq., for Respondent Kathy Zalewski

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Respondent and Cross-Complainant Katherine A. Zalewski's Motion To Impose Issue Sanctions, Evidentiary Sanctions, And Monetary Sanctions Against Petitioner Charles Bergeron is granted as it seeks monetary sanctions. The court finds that the late responses and previous failure to participate in discovery were misuses of the discovery process. Sanctions are imposed against Petitioner in the amount of $900, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Off Calendar Dec-05-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearance:
Richard H. Harris, Jr., for Katherine Zalewski, Respondent/Moving Party

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Respondent Katherine Zalweski's Motion To Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories, Production Of Documents (Sets One and Two) is granted as it seeks further responses to Form Interrogatory 17.1 as to Requests For Admission Nos. 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 7, 8, 10, and 16; and inspection and copying of certain original documents. Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order. The court finds that the incomplete responses were a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are imposed against Petitioner Charles Bergeron in the amount of $675, to be paid within 20 days of notice of this order. The previous order, entered on October 12, 2006 is vacated, and this order shall be entered in its place. Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
